name: Srivatsa et al. Proxy Student IRT Evaluation Extract
population_or_context: Mathematics and reading comprehension items from NAEP used to compare LLM proxy students with real student populations.
sample_size: The ACL Anthology abstract reports 489 NAEP items and thousands of real student responses, with 11 LLMs tested under several prompting strategies.
methods: Extracted the psychometric quality-evaluation strategy that maps model and real-student response patterns onto the same IRT ability scale.
education_context: NAEP mathematics and reading comprehension item responses across subjects and grades.
response_origin: LLM proxy-student responses are compared with real student response patterns from NAEP populations.
rubric_label_space: NAEP item response outcomes used for IRT ability estimation.
label_source: Real NAEP responses and IRT calibration provide the comparator scale; LLM responses are model-generated.
generation_model: Eleven LLMs are tested with several prompting strategies.
generation_method: LLMs are prompted as proxy students to answer NAEP items in mathematics and reading comprehension.
prompt_strategy: Several prompting strategies are evaluated rather than one fixed prompt design.
evaluation_method: Model and real student response patterns are mapped onto the same IRT ability scale and compared across subjects and grades.
scorer_use: Proxy-student simulation quality evaluation; not automated scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: Thousands of real NAEP student responses provide the population comparator.
reported_metrics: IRT ability-scale alignment and response-pattern comparison across 489 NAEP items, 11 LLMs, and multiple prompting strategies.
limitations: The reported conclusion is negative: no model-prompt combination reliably simulates real student ability patterns across subjects and grades.
The evidence is bounded to NAEP mathematics and reading comprehension items.
IRT alignment evaluates ability-pattern fidelity, not open-response realism or scorer utility.
